articles.title: Acadeca Takes Over the State
articles.author: The Railsplitter Staff
articles.dek: On January 24, Lincoln High School’s Academic Decathlon team competed in the first portion of regional competition. Academic Decathlon (ACADECA) is an academic competition in which students study ten…
articles.body:
On January 24, Lincoln High School’s Academic Decathlon team competed in the first portion of regional competition. Academic Decathlon (ACADECA) is an academic competition in which students study ten subjects under one overarching theme. This year’s topic is the Roaring 20’s, an era of jazz, economic change, and cultural rebellion.

Competitors are tested subjectively in math, social science, science, literature, music, art, and economics, and objectively through speech, interview, essay, and the Super Quiz. At our school, ACADECA is run by Magnet Coordinator Ms. Kwan and AP Seminar teacher Ms. Crabtree, who guide students from the moment the official resources are released in May all the way through competition season.

The team is divided into groups based on GPA: Honors (3.8+): Ivan Chi, Yanna Kwong, Tiffany Nguyen Scholastic (3.5–3.8): Abby Guerra, Jiali Huang, Tira Cam Varsity (3.0–3.4): Andre Chen, Yawen Liang, Jieya Liu The JV team consists of Sandra Portillo, Xinyue Huang, David Lin, Jachym Taussig.

The ACADECA competition took place at Garfield High School, where students gathered in a large room filled with competitors from other schools. Each academic test lasted 30 minutes, with approximately 50 questions per subject. Later, students faced one of the most personal parts of the competition: interviews and speeches, where judges evaluated not just knowledge, but confidence, communication, and character.

When asked about making it to state, Abby didn’t hesitate. “Of course. The team has prepared rigorously and with all their might.” But for Abby, state isn’t just about medals. “State is going to be really fun. Last year, everyone was friends with everyone. It felt like a family. There was love all around. It brought us closer, and we kept hanging out all year."

As the team looks ahead to state competitions later this year, the coaches and students alike are excited not just for the chance to win, but for one more opportunity to learn, grow, and experience something special together.

“I have full confidence in our team securing some medals,” said Scholastic member Abby Guerra. “Jiali is the second highest scorer in all of LAUSD from scrimmages alone.”

articles.title: Pi Day—A Never-Ending Joy to the Math-Mind
articles.author: Jada Guevara
articles.dek: On March 13th, to celebrate Pi Day, MST Magnet planned a competition to see who could count the most digits in the infinite number of Pi. The winner, who counted up to 153 digits was Jiayu Li. She is…
articles.body:
On March 13th, to celebrate Pi Day, MST Magnet planned a competition to see who could count the most digits in the infinite number of Pi. The winner, who counted up to 153 digits was Jiayu Li. She is a ninth grader and participated on behalf of Ms. Sara’s advisory class.

She was proud to be the Pi winner, but felt she could have recited more and continued to reach a higher goal. She said, “I originally remembered 400 digits, but was stopped by the judges due to a misconception of getting a digit wrong.” She prepared in many ways and one thing she mentioned was that she prepared by using a song focused on Pi. She chose to do this competition because before high school she was the president of her middle school.

She participated in her school competitions and wanted to give it a try at Lincoln. Previously she got to 150 digits and this year she passed her record by 3 digits. She hopes she will do better and go higher next year. After this event, and during lunch, the teachers and ASB set up a “pie in the face" event where many teachers participated and all the students had fun.

Pi is the special number when you divide any circle’s circumference (distance around a circle) by its diameter (distance across). This number is infinite, but what makes it special is that it doesn’t repeat. Pi is an infinite number, up till this day math solvers are trying to find the concluding digit of pi. Pi is crucial in everyday lives. For instance, engineering, architecture, technology, space exploration, and more use Pi. So, if you want to participate next year, just look at a circle and count 3.1415926….

articles.title: Strike a Pose: Prom Fashion Show
articles.author: Sophia Miranda
articles.dek: With prom season upon us, our senior class has thought of a great way to sneak peak what the prom fashions will look like this year. With the prom theme this year being “Upper East Side New York", the…
articles.body:
With prom season upon us, our senior class has thought of a great way to sneak peak what the prom fashions will look like this year. With the prom theme this year being “Upper East Side New York", the prom fashion show is to show off our beautiful and handsome senior class dressed up in their prom attire.

With their beautiful dresses and suits we are sure to be in for a high fashion prom. The light and music set-up simulated a real fashion show one might see in the Upper East Side. Coordinated by our senior cabinet committee, Senior Class president Ivy Chieng said, “It gave me a different perspective on how seniors feel about prom and how this show excited the senior class to want to go to prom and it was very heartwarming to hear such positives comments about our show and we as a cabinet committee were very pleased with the positive feedback from everyone.”

With the show happening every year the senior class is committed to making a great experience for the seniors to excite them for their special night. Excitement for prom is through the roof, and it’s sure to deliver.

“I think the show went very well, and we had a great turn out and we were so happy to have so many people involved.” - Senior Noel Mendoza

articles.title: The Real Life Mascot
articles.author: Sophia Miranda
articles.dek: What is orange, furry and has Tiger spirit? Be on the lookout for what people are calling our real life mascot…the little tiger cat.
articles.body:
What is orange, furry and has Tiger spirit? Be on the lookout for what people are calling our real life mascot…the little tiger cat.

A little orange cat has been found living on campus and taking its morning laps around the school. The staff and students take turns giving the kitty some treats. It is truly a cute site to see to brighten up your day when walking into school. So get to school bright and early to catch a glimpse of our little mascot.

articles.title: Tiger Baseball Comes Roaring Back
articles.author: The Railsplitter Staff
articles.dek: Lincoln bounced back after a loss on the road last Friday to Fremont. They blew past the Santee Falcons 9-1 on Thursday. That large 9-1 mark stands out as the most commanding margin for the Tigers yet…
articles.body: Lincoln bounced back after a loss on the road last Friday to Fremont. They blew past the Santee Falcons 9-1 on Thursday. That large 9-1 mark stands out as the most commanding margin for the Tigers yet this season. Paul Martinez and Andrew Ayon were standout players at the plate. Paul went 2-for-4 with three RBI, one stolen base, and one run, while Andrew earned two runs and four stolen bases. Andrew has now snagged at least one in each of the last four games he's played. Another player making a difference was Bryant Guitron, who went 1-for-2 with two RBI. Lincoln now has a record of 3-4. However, on Friday, April 10, Lincoln came up short against Marshall and fell 5-1.

articles.title: World Baseball Classic: Team USA loses 3-2 in a dramatic final in Miami.
articles.author: Ruby Campos
articles.dek: Venezuela won their first-ever World Baseball Classic (WBC) title in 2026, defeating Team USA 3-2 in a dramatic final in Miami. The World Baseball Classic showcased the best of international baseball…
articles.body:
Venezuela won their first-ever World Baseball Classic (WBC) title in 2026, defeating Team USA 3-2 in a dramatic final in Miami. The World Baseball Classic showcased the best of international baseball and brought together the best 20 national teams from around the world to compete in one tournament. The competitions were held from March 5th to the 17th across Tokyo, San Juan, Houston, and Miami. The WBC featured some of the best players from in the MLB as well as seventeen year olds playing for the first time.

The competition is divided into 4 different stages. The first stage of the tournament is called pool play where the 20 teams are split into 4 pools of 5. The teams that played in Pool A were Puerto Rico, Cuba, Canada, Panama, Colombia and they all played in San Juan. Pool B had teams like USA, Italy, Mexico, Great Britain, Brazil and played in Houston. Pool C had Japan, Netherlands, Australia, Korea, Czechia and played in Japan. Lastly pool D had Dominican Republic, Venezuela, Israel, Nicaragua, Chinese Taipei playing in Miami. Ultimately pool play determines which teams will advance. Each team faces every other team one time in their group and only the top two teams from each pool moves on based on wins, and tie breakers are determined by who has the best run differential, so it’s important to score more runs to advance.

The second stage of the WBC was the quarterfinals where the eight teams that advanced played in a single elimination game. With matchups like the Dominican Republic vs. South Korea, the United States vs. Canada, Italy vs. Puerto Rico, and Japan vs. Venezuela. The Dominican Republic and the USA moved on as expected. While Venezuela shocked the world by defeating the defending champions Japan. Italy also kept their surprising winning streak alive by winning their match ups.

The third stage of the WBC is the semifinals where the remaining 4 teams played each other to see who will advance to the championship game. Venezuela continued its historic streak by defeating Italy to finally advance to their first WBC final ever. Meanwhile, the USA beat the Dominican Republic in a close match to earn its 3rd straight trip to the championship game.

In the final stage of the WBC Venezuela stunned the USA with a 3-2 victory at LoanDepot Park in Miami to win its first WBC title ever. Venezuela looked to lead early on and the USA did briefly tie the game late, but Venezuela had a key go ahead rbi in the 9th inning giving them the win. The win marked a historic moment for Venezuela baseball and capped an exciting tournament full of upsets and dramatic moments.

articles.title: Boys Volleyball Gets the W
articles.author: The Railsplitter Staff
articles.dek: Lincoln lost to Wilson back in March and unfortunately they wound up with the same result on Thursday. The Tigers fell just short of the Mules by a score of 3-2. The Tigers have now taken an 'L' in ba…
articles.body:
Lincoln lost to Wilson back in March and unfortunately they wound up with the same result on Thursday. The Tigers fell just short of the Mules by a score of 3-2. The Tigers have now taken an 'L' in back-to-back matches. Lincoln took a 2-1 lead headed into the fourth set, but sadly they lost the next two. The match finished with set scores of 25-23, 23-25, 18-25, 25-16, 15-13.

It was a great game when Lincoln took on Franklin on Thursday. The Tigers walked away with a 3-1 victory over the Panthers. The Tigers haven't had any issues with the Panthers recently, as the game was their sixth consecutive win against them. While Lincoln might've won, it sure wasn't easy: Franklin scored at least 20 points in every set they played. The final score came out to 25-22, 23-25, 25-23, 25-20. Lincoln now has a winning record of 6-4.

articles.title: Track Takes the Field
articles.author: Isabella Brown
articles.dek: The 2026 track and field season has officially begun. Coach Rattay is the head coach for sprinters and shot putters, and Coach Subuyuj coaches long distance. The entire track and field team consists o…
articles.body:
The 2026 track and field season has officially begun. Coach Rattay is the head coach for sprinters and shot putters, and Coach Subuyuj coaches long distance. The entire track and field team consists of about 60 athletes.

Coach Rattay has been coaching at Lincoln for many years and in different sports as well. He is passionate in what he does, and encourages others to do their best, simultaneously gaining confidence. Coach Rattay has noted that the team has gotten smaller as the season went on. He has lost some of his best sprinters. This does not stop Coach Rattay from inspiring his runners to improve and challenge themselves.

Coach Subuyuj has consistently motivated his students to strive for higher times and even some that seem impossible. There have been two preseason track meets, and one official track meet. The coaches as well as the athletes say they are most excited to compete against Wilson because they are the rival school, but also Eagle Rock because they bring competition. Another competitive school, Bravo, has a very large and competitive long distance team, and our distance team is excited to compete against them.

Layla has shown that she is capable of achieving faster times and being a team player, helping out her fellow teammates. Keiana Douglas is only a freshman and most definitely a talented runner. She competes with other varsity athletes in the hurdle races and does extremely well. Diego Salgado is one of the fastest long distance runners, won many of his races, and is recognized by his coaches. It is highly suggested that everyone should go to a track meet and support all Lincoln athletes.

There are three players being spotlighted for their incredible hard work and training: Layla Velazquez, Keiana Douglas and Diego Salgado.

articles.title: BTS/Kpop Comeback
articles.author: Miranda Garcia
articles.dek: BTS, a South Korean KPOP band had a comeback on March 20 2026 after not playing with all seven members in over five years. The reason for the absence was due to the military service required in South…
articles.body:
BTS, a South Korean KPOP band had a comeback on March 20 2026 after not playing with all seven members in over five years. The reason for the absence was due to the military service required in South Korea.

The Album has 14 songs which each have different genres, including pop-rock, pop-rap, trap and R&B. However, it has also caused some controversy because it doesn’t seem “like BTS” to some fanatics. One of the most criticized songs is ‘Swim’ and ‘Fiya’.

Although it is criticized, the album has some hits that are pretty good such as the song “They Don’t Know ‘Bout Us”.

articles.title: The Decline of Console Gaming
articles.author: Yew Yu
articles.dek: When it comes to gaming, Microsoft is widely considered to be one of the most well known gaming console companies in the world, having produced multiple generational consoles like the Xbox.
articles.body:
When it comes to gaming, Microsoft is widely considered to be one of the most well known gaming console companies in the world, having produced multiple generational consoles like the Xbox.

When a new CEO for Microsoft Gaming was announced in February 2026, the subjective decision to “sunset” Xbox to focus heavily on AI was revealed. Some people are not happy with this change, with some saying it is just another cash grab, while others are sad about the “end of an era”.

Around 31% of Lincoln students use console gaming, while the majority games on PCs. Console gaming is definitely starting to die out as it is replaced by more modern gaming solutions.

articles.title: Are Video Games Losing Their Allure?
articles.author: Rebecca Medrano
articles.dek: It’s no secret that Epic games is losing players due to their many unnecessary collaborations with Fortnite in hopes of gaining more funds off their player base. Fortnite isn’t gaining users as much a…
articles.body:
It’s no secret that Epic games is losing players due to their many unnecessary collaborations with Fortnite in hopes of gaining more funds off their player base. Fortnite isn’t gaining users as much as they usually do and with their recent choice to hike up prices for v-bucks, an in game currency, what was once originally 1,000 v-bucks has lowered to 800 v-bucks all for the same price.

People are in outrage because previously players with the Crew Pass once earned 1,000 v-bucks every month but the price of v-bucks has dropped to 800. In addition, the seasonal battle passes no longer give people the same amount of rewards. V-bucks inflation is a huge cause of many players quitting the game.

Recent updates include collaborations with bands, other video games, musicians, television shows, and much more. You’d think people would be happy with all of these collaborations but no--quite the opposite in fact! Players have been complaining about them.

Epic games isn't the only company on a downward spiral. Popular mobile game Roblox is completely destroying their game updates disguised as safety but it is now becoming controversial. Players of Roblox are in disarray with the recent “chat ban” in order to prevent grooming, however with the mandatory face scan to verify age that is so easily broken through. Pople wonder just how seriously Roblox is attempting to act.

Roblox has also made claims to remove items such as classic faces, classic clothes, r15 mode, and even completely remove stiff bodies for the sake of the more “dynamic” clothes and avatars, and people are outraged by all the changes and have protested to no avail. Roblox only bent to the whims of their community after facing a huge drop in profit.

Other gaming companies are on the rise. CAPCOM recently skyrocketed in popularity and sales due to their most recently published game Resident Evil 9 Requiem. The Resident Evil franchise is well known for their characters but beyond that the game features in depth lore and world building that most other games don't do, or put little to no effort into due to something called “second-screening”.

All in all, video games aren't really losing their appeal. Players are just getting tired of the unfair changes and updates. Games that focus on fun, creativity, and listening to their community are still successful. In the end, players will stick with games that treat them fairly.

articles.title: What Annoys You?
articles.author: Samantha Ortiz
articles.dek: A pet peeve refers to an annoying behavior or action that one finds irritating more than others. Some believe that the root of a pet peeve grows out of a violation of social expectations.
articles.body:
A pet peeve refers to an annoying behavior or action that one finds irritating more than others. Some believe that the root of a pet peeve grows out of a violation of social expectations.

School can be an expansion ground for pet peeves on account of the fact that people follow different behaviors that others may not be pleased with. A student expressed that their biggest pet peeve in school is when students walk at a slow pace in the hall. They further revealed that this irritated them because such behavior “blocks their path to class”.

Students also shared that the “no cellphone policy” has become rather annoying as well. One student in particular found it bothersome how administration “freaks out” about phones being out in class, especially when there are more important issues to detail with on a daily basis.

articles.title: Fake News: Can You Tell the Difference?
articles.author: Jennifer Month - Hernandez
articles.dek: Today it is hard to discern real information from fake news. Fake news can lead to chaos and confusion. The ability to distinguish real information from fake news has become crucial.
articles.body:
Today it is hard to discern real information from fake news. Fake news can lead to chaos and confusion. The ability to distinguish real information from fake news has become crucial.

Case in point is a bizarre story of a man named Ricky Hollowell who was allegedly kidnapped for 3 days by a dolphin named “Gerold “ off the coast of Fort Myers beach near Sanibel Causeway Florida. Although this is an interesting story, this is also entirely fabricated information by a fakebook account page called “The dark humor report”.

Another event that viralized was the alleged claim about the alleged death of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s, this case also through social media and was circulated world wide, but the Israeli government officially dismissed it as fake news. About 86% of global online citizens are exposed to false news online.

At Lincoln through a survey conducted by The Railsplitter 44% of 50 students surveyed believed that the story of Ricky Hallowell may have happened. Out of 73 students surveyed 50.7% of the students claim they check to see if the information they see on social media is real or not.

articles.title: Spring Things to Do in LA
articles.author: Audrie Ozuna
articles.dek: Hey Tigers! Spring is here — here are some currently trending things you can do around Los Angeles.
articles.body:
Hey Tigers! Spring is here which means the weather is getting a little warmer and closer to summer. So as the flowers bloom, here are some currently trending things you can do!

Start with a trip to a nice cafe — try Community Goods, Olive and James Cafe Tea in West Hollywood; Memory Look, DAMO, Rok, and Stagger in Koreatown; or Archives Of Us in Chinatown for an iced matcha latte.

After that, visit The Academy Museum of Motion Picture. It is free entry for students — just bring your student ID. Great if you have an interest in movies, film, or behind the scenes.

If that's not your vibe, check out the Getty Museum or Getty Villa. One overlooks the coast, the other the westside of Los Angeles. Both are free entry — you only pay for parking.
